student_message: Right down my nose and tummy!
final_message:
You got it! A line straight down your nose and tummy divides your body into two matching halves, which is a perfect example of symmetry. You are doing so great! 

Now, Earth to Earthling, let’s be mirror detectives! 🕵️‍♂️ Imagine a cool robot face with a perfect line of symmetry straight down the middle. If the left side has one glowing red eye and two laser antennae, what exactly do you think is on the right side? 🤖

orchestrator_intent.goal: Validate her symmetry example and re-engage her visual attention.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student correctly identified a line of symmetry on her body, but her gaze has been away for 4 turns. Dialogue will validate her answer, and engagement will prompt her to look back at the screen.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
